title: RIO Intake Schema v1
description: Standard intake structure for all RIO governed actions. Every request entering the system MUST conform to this schema. Defined by Brian K. Rasmussen. Flow: Identity → Intent → Context → Authorization → Execution → Receipt → Ledger.

identity.description: WHO is requesting. Establishes the human or agent identity behind this action.

subject.description: Unique identifier for the requesting entity (e.g., 'brian.k.rasmussen' or 'agent:manus')

email.description: Email address associated with the identity

role.description: Role in the RIO system. Owner has full authority. Agent acts on behalf of owner. Delegate has limited authority. Viewer is read-only.

on_behalf_of.description: If an agent is acting on behalf of a human, the human's subject ID goes here. Creates the chain: Human → Agent → Action.

intent.description: WHAT action is being requested. The specific operation the system should perform.

target.description: Primary target of the action (e.g., email address, file path, repo name)

parameters.description: Action-specific parameters. Schema varies by action type.

context.description: WHY this action is being requested. Provides the governance layer with information to evaluate risk and apply policy.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why this action is needed

risk_scope.description: Category of risk this action falls under

source_session.description: Session or conversation ID where this intent originated

related_intents.description: IDs of related previous intents (for multi-step workflows)

authorization.description: APPROVAL decision. Null until a human or delegation policy approves/denies. Filled by the governance pipeline, not by the requester.

decided_by.description: Who made the decision (human subject ID or 'delegation_policy:<id>')

signature.description: Ed25519 signature of the intent hash, proving the approver authorized this specific action

decided_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the decision was made

reason.description: Optional reason for approval or denial
